preview_text:
The model favors Belgium with a 65% win probability, leaving New Zealand at 11% and a draw at 24%. Belgium enters as a clear favorite, though the model assigns the underdog roughly one chance in nine to secure victory.

The 54-percentage-point gap between Belgium's win probability and New Zealand's reflects a heavily asymmetric matchup. A draw at 24% sits as a meaningful outcome—more likely than either team winning outright except Belgium—suggesting the model sees competitive balance in the early-game window, even if Belgium's quality tilts the path to victory strongly in their favor.

The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket both assign Belgium a higher win probability than the model, at 73% and 76% respectively, while compressing the draw odds to 17% and 16%. The divergence widens at the margins: books price New Zealand tighter at 10% (down from 11%) and are notably less confident in a stalemate. No consensus has emerged strongly between model and market on which reading is correct.
